description: Full prediction lifecycle log. Every prediction lives here from creation to resolution. Day-by-day log entries are added during each briefing session. RCA is written on WRONG outcomes. Framework updates are proposed here and promoted to signal-framework.json on approval.

predictions.prediction: SWIGGY will breach ₹260 within 4 weeks as selling pressure and weak quick-commerce fundamentals persist
predictions.thesis: SWIGGY is within 2% of 52-week low at ₹259.15. FII outflows of ₹11,163 Cr on Mar 30 suggest continued selling. No fundamental catalyst for reversal before Q4 results (May 8). Brent at $115 increases delivery cost pressure. Iran-driven market risk-off adds to selling.

daily_log.note: 52-week low breached. SWIGGY closed at ₹259.25 vs low of ₹259.15. Prediction confirmed 26 days ahead of check date.
signal_updates: T001 confirmed — 52-wk low breach
M003 confirmed — FII sold ₹11,163 Cr

predictions.prediction: SWIGGY will NOT breach ₹260 by April 8 — will hold above on DII support
predictions.thesis: DII inflows of ₹14,895 Cr on Mar 30 suggested domestic support could hold SWIGGY above key level short-term.

daily_log.note: WRONG — SWIGGY breached ₹260 same day as prediction was made. DII support was insufficient against FII magnitude.
signal_updates: M003 missed — FII outflow ₹11,163 Cr overwhelmed DII inflow

rca.root_cause: Checked DII inflows (bullish signal) but did not check FII outflow magnitude. When FII outflow is >1.5x DII inflow, short-term support predictions are unreliable.
rca.missing_factor: FII/DII flow ratio — absolute magnitude of FII outflow vs DII inflow
rca.signal_missed: M003 — FII net sold ₹11,163 Cr vs DII bought ₹14,895 Cr. Ratio was 0.75x but absolute FII selling was extreme.
rca.framework_gap: Signal framework checks FII and DII flows independently but does not flag when FII selling is in panic-mode (>₹8,000 Cr single day).

proposed_factor.name: FII Single-Day Panic Threshold
proposed_factor.description: Flag when FII single-day net sell exceeds ₹8,000 Cr — invalidates short-term bullish predictions
proposed_factor.causal_explanation: At extreme FII outflow levels (>₹8,000 Cr/day), domestic institutional buying cannot offset intraday price pressure regardless of DII net positive. Creates supply shock.
proposed_factor.evidence: P007 miss — ₹11,163 Cr FII sell overwhelmed ₹14,895 Cr DII buy

rca.lesson: For short-horizon bullish predictions, always check M003 absolute magnitude, not just direction. Panic-level FII selling negates support.

predictions.prediction: Nifty 50 will close above 22,600 on April 2 — FY-end forced selling exhaustion + FY27 fresh start buying
predictions.thesis: FY26 closed with Nifty -2.14% on March 30 (forced institutional selling for book closure). Historical pattern: FY-start sessions see relief bounce as selling exhausts and SIPs trigger fresh inflows.
predictions.outcome_evidence: NSE official close April 2. Closed 113 pts above target. FY-end exhaustion thesis confirmed.

predictions.prediction: MRPL will fall to ₹160–170 range by May 28 as Brent sustained above $110 compresses refining margins
predictions.thesis: Brent at $115 with Hormuz blockade = sustained crude input cost elevation. MRPL's GRM spread compresses when crude is above $110 for extended periods. No near-term refinery upgrade catalyst.

daily_log.note: Brent fell to $113 on April 1 on de-escalation hopes, but Iran rejected US peace plan same day. Crude remains above $110. Thesis intact.
signal_updates: M001 — Brent still elevated, thesis on track

daily_log.note: Brent holding $105–107 range. Slightly below ₹110 threshold. Monitor: if sustained below $100, MRPL may not fall to target range.
signal_updates: M001 — Brent slightly easing, slight risk to thesis

daily_log.note: Trump's April 6 deadline on Strait of Hormuz passed. Brent at $108.3 as of Apr 5-6. De-escalation narrative strengthening, crude below $110 threshold. MRPL had trading pause (results window). Thesis weakening — need to reassess if crude stays below $110.
signal_updates: M001 — Brent now below $110, increasing risk to thesis
Geopolitical: De-escalation narrative emerging

daily_log.note: Brent at $110.05 — BACK ABOVE $110 threshold. Apr 6 de-escalation narrative was FALSE: Iran REJECTED 45-day ceasefire proposal, demanding permanent peace instead. Trump issued NEW deadline: Tuesday 8pm ET (Apr 8) for Hormuz reopening or massive escalation. War premium RETURNING. Additionally, govt imposed export tax on diesel this week — direct GRM compression for refiners. MRPL at ~₹175 (above ₹160-170 target but thesis is REVIVING). Confidence recovering: from 1 back to 2.
signal_updates: M001 — Brent $110.05, back above thesis threshold
Geopolitical: Iran REJECTED ceasefire, Trump new deadline Tue 8pm ET
Macro: Govt diesel export tax adds direct refiner margin pressure

predictions.prediction: Nifty 50 will test 22,000–22,200 support zone by May 10 as Iran war premium sustains selling
predictions.thesis: Iran war creating sustained FII outflow + crude premium. Nifty at 22,819 has technical support at 22,000–22,200 (200DMA zone). Expect test within 2 months.

daily_log.note: Nifty at 22,713 on April 2. Already -1% from prediction start price. Iran war ongoing, thesis intact. April 2 bounce may slow descent but 22,000 test likely by May.
signal_updates: M003 — FII still net sellers (₹9,931 Cr on Apr 2)
T002 — approaching support levels

daily_log.note: MAJOR REVERSAL: Nifty rallied to 22,968 on April 6 (+255 pts, +1.12% from Apr 2). De-escalation narrative replacing war premium. Index now 768 points ABOVE target zone of 22,000–22,200. This contradicts thesis. If de-escalation holds, thesis may fail to resolve.
signal_updates: M001 — Geopolitical premium deflating as Trump deadline passes without escalation
M003 — FII data for Apr 6 pending, but sentiment shifted to de-escalation relief
Thesis contradiction — Nifty moving away from support test zone

daily_log.note: THESIS REVIVING: Apr 6 de-escalation rally was a DEAD CAT BOUNCE. Iran REJECTED 45-day ceasefire, Trump set new Tuesday 8pm ET Hormuz deadline. Nifty opened at 22,838 — already DOWN 130 pts from Apr 6 close. FII sold ₹8,167 Cr on Apr 6 (CONFIRMED PAT001 threshold >₹8,000 Cr — second data point). War premium returning. Nifty 638 pts above target zone (22,000-22,200) — but with PAT001 1.5x multiplier applied, effective downside estimate extends to 21,500-21,800. Services PMI hit 14-month low in March — macro headwind adding. Confidence recovering: 1 → 3.
signal_updates: M001 — War premium resurging (Iran rejected ceasefire, Trump escalation threat)
M003 — FII sold ₹8,167 Cr Apr 6 (PAT001 threshold: >₹8,000 Cr BREACHED)
T002 — Nifty pulling back from Apr 6 relief rally
Macro: India services PMI 14-month low March

predictions.prediction: Coal India will hold above ₹430 through May 28 on e-auction premium + power demand
predictions.thesis: Coal e-auction prices elevated on power demand surge. Renewables not scaling fast enough to offset coal dependency. PSU dividend yield provides price floor.

daily_log.note: Coal India at ₹451 (+30.7% from March close). Already well above ₹430 target. Thesis confirming ahead of schedule.
signal_updates: S008 — Coal demand strong, price elevated well above target

daily_log.note: Coal India at ₹461.25 — up 1.48% on April 6, gained ₹11.89 from opening. Thesis strongly confirming. Now ₹31.25 above target with 52 days remaining. Resilient to geopolitical risk. Only risk: broad PSU sector crash if Nifty breaks 22,000.
signal_updates: S008 — Continued strength in coal e-auction segment
M006 — PSU rerating ongoing, dividend yield floor holding

daily_log.note: Market weak open (Nifty 22,838, Sensex -0.41%). Coal India expected to hold steady — domestic demand thesis fully insulated from Iran/crude dynamics. No Coal India-specific negative news. Target >₹430, current ~₹461 — thesis tracking 7.2% above target with 51 days to go. Risk: Escalating war premium causing correlated PSU selloff if Nifty cracks 22,200. Maintain confidence at 5. Watch: IREDA earnings Apr 16 may spur PSU sector rotation which could affect Coal India.
signal_updates: S008 — Coal demand fundamentals unchanged
M006 — PSU rerating theme intact, dividend yield floor supportive

rca_pattern_log.description: Short-term bullish predictions fail when FII single-day sell > ₹8,000 Cr, regardless of DII support
rca_pattern_log.notes: Apr 6: FII sold ₹8,167 Cr (threshold: >₹8,000 Cr). Second data point supporting PAT001. Pattern is ACTIVE for risk estimates — applying 1.5x multiplier to downside on Apr 7 Nifty. Still need a formal WRONG prediction with PAT001 root cause to promote to framework (requires 2 occurrences). Apr 7 session will be the test — if P003 thesis gets further confirmation today, PAT001 may be promoted in EOD wrap.

last_threshold_event.pattern_note: PAT001 applies more to NEXT DAY reversal risk than same-day — Nifty fell at open Apr 7 confirming delayed effect
